---
title: "LRPHS02: Regularized-horseshoe ranking cross-check - word-reading level"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPHS02 is the level companion to LRPHS01: an **independent Bayesian sensitivity
check** on the gradient-boosting predictor ranking for word-reading level (GB
`lrpgbl12`). A regularized (Finnish) horseshoe sparse regression regresses the
concurrent word-reading count on the standardised same-wave levels of the other
constructs (plus age), with a subject random intercept for the repeated
timepoints; predictors are ranked by posterior `P(|beta| > delta)` and compared to
the GB *cluster* ranking at construct level.

_Ranking cross-check, NOT causal._ Agreement with the gradient-boosting order is reassurance the ranking is not a
tree-method artefact — not a causal claim. Every coefficient is an adjusted
association; the randomised causal effect lives in the ITT suite.

_Concurrent level, n ~ 215 over 54 children._ One row per child × timepoint (`phase_mode="levels"`) with a subject random
intercept. A high naive association is partly same-wave same-construct
correlation, so read the *ordering* as exploratory. Hyperparameters are
weakly-informative priors, not tuned.

## Model

Beta-Binomial concurrent word-reading count with a subject random intercept
`u_child` and standardised same-wave predictor levels entering under a
regularized-horseshoe prior (global scale `tau`, per-predictor local scale
`lambda_k`, slab `c^2`):

$$
\eta_{it} = \alpha + u_{\text{child}[i]} + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},it}
        + \sum_k \beta_k\, z_{k,it},
\qquad
\beta_k \sim \mathcal{N}\!\big(0,\ \tau\, \tilde{\lambda}_k\big).
$$

Construct predictor set: the concurrent non-floored measure levels (letter sounds
`L`, receptive/expressive vocabulary `R`/`E`, blending `B`, basic concepts `F`,
receptive grammar `T`) plus age. The RLI-blocks / behaviour covariates are omitted
here: they are wave-sparse and requiring them would collapse the levels frame to
one row per child, removing the repeated-measures structure the subject random
intercept needs.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_horseshoe.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
